expectations.evidence: CSS uses --_chip-* intermediate variables (12 total) on .chip root, role-agnostic: --_chip-fg-bold, --_chip-fg-bold-high, --_chip-bg-subtle, --_chip-default-high, --_chip-default-accent-a11y, etc.
All intermediates map to --Primary-* with legacy fallbacks: e.g. --_chip-fg-bold: var(--Primary-FG-Bold, var(--Surface-Bold))

.filled uses --_chip-fg-bold for bg + --_chip-fg-bold-high for text. .tinted uses --_chip-bg-subtle bg + --_chip-default-accent-a11y text. .outlined uses transparent bg + --_chip-default-low-stroke border.
.appearanceNeutral and .appearanceNegative classes each remap all 12 intermediate variables to their role tokens (--Neutral-*, --Negative-*)
Explanation document covers the four-layer architecture, CSS cascade walkthrough, bold inversion explanation, and states 'the chip adapts with zero JavaScript'
File header states 'ZERO LITERALS — all values come from design tokens' and inspection confirms all colors use var() references
